answers: Node.js lets you run JavaScript on the server. Combined with Express.js, you can build full backend APIs, handle databases, and serve web apps.
Node.js basics: npm (package manager), Express.js (web framework), middleware, routing, REST APIs, and connecting to MongoDB or PostgreSQL.
Learn Node.js: know JavaScript first, then follow the official docs or Traversy Media's crash course. Build a REST API as your first project.
Express.js is the most popular Node.js framework. Install with npm install express, create routes, handle requests, and you have a backend in minutes!
